{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Customer Service Officer",
  "description": "TNL Pharma Limited is a group of registered companies, with operational basethroughout Nigeria and Corporate Head Offices in Lagos, Nigeria.Established in September 1997, TNL Pharma Limited is a member of the PharmaceuticalManufacturing and Marketing Companies in Nigeria. It is also a member ofseveral trade bodies such as World Trade Organisation (WTO), Nigerian GermanTrade Delegation and Nigerian Turkish Chamber of Commerce.Our Manufacturing plants are located in Lagos for both Pharmaceuticals and Neutraceuticals.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Officer

Job Locations: Igando - Lagos &amp; Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full -time

Job Summary&nbsp;


	We are seeking for a friendly and customer-focused individual to join our sales company as a Customer Service Officer.
	The ideal candidate will handle customer inquiries, resolve complaints, and support the sales team to ensure excellent customer satisfaction and smooth operations.


Key Responsibilities


	Respond promptly to customer inquiries via phone, email, or in person.
	Resolve customer complaints and provide appropriate solutions.
	Support the sales team with order processing and client follow-ups.
	Maintain accurate records of customer interactions.
	Provide information about company products and services.


Requirements


	HND / BSC in any field
	1&ndash;2 years of experience in customer service, preferably in pharmaceutical or medical sales environment.
	Excellent communication, interpersonal, and problem-solving skills.
	Proficient in Microsoft Excel, Word, and other applications.
	Ability to multitask, prioritize, and work under pressure.
	Attention to detail and high level of accuracy in documentation.
	Professionalism and ability to interact effectively with healthcare professionals.
	Knowledge of pharmaceutical products, healthcare systems, or medical terminology is an added advantage.


&nbsp;Salary
&nbsp;N120,000 - N150,000 per month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: tnlrecruitment01@gmail.com using the Job Position as the suject of the mail.",

  "validThrough": "2026-05-20",

  },

      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },

Customer Service Officer Job at TNL Pharma Limited




        
    

        
        TNL Pharma Limited is a group of registered companies, with operational basethroughout Nigeria and Corporate Head Offices in Lagos, Nigeria.Established in September 1997, TNL Pharma Limited is a member of the PharmaceuticalManufacturing and Marketing Companies in Nigeria. It is also a member ofseveral trade bodies such as World Trade Organisation (WTO), Nigerian GermanTrade Delegation and Nigerian Turkish Chamber of Commerce.Our Manufacturing plants are located in Lagos for both Pharmaceuticals and Neutraceuticals.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Officer

Job Locations: Igando - Lagos & Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full -time
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Summary 


	We are seeking for a friendly and customer-focused individual to join our sales company as a Customer Service Officer.
	The ideal candidate will handle customer inquiries, resolve complaints, and support the sales team to ensure excellent customer satisfaction and smooth operations.


Key Responsibilities


	Respond promptly to customer inquiries via phone, email, or in person.
	Resolve customer complaints and provide appropriate solutions.
	Support the sales team with order processing and client follow-ups.
	Maintain accurate records of customer interactions.
	Provide information about company products and services.


Requirements


	HND / BSC in any field
	1–2 years of experience in customer service, preferably in pharmaceutical or medical sales environment.
	Excellent communication, interpersonal, and problem-solving skills.
	Proficient in Microsoft Excel, Word, and other applications.
	Ability to multitask, prioritize, and work under pressure.
	Attention to detail and high level of accuracy in documentation.
	Professionalism and ability to interact effectively with healthcare professionals.
	Knowledge of pharmaceutical products, healthcare systems, or medical terminology is an added advantage.


 Salary
 N120,000 - N150,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the suject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "HR & Admin Manager",
  "description": "Pause Factory is a business and management consulting company that leverages people&rsquo;s emotions and performance architecture to solve organizational challenges and craft organizational transformation. We provide innovative corporate training solutions, recruitment, HR outsourcing, organizational assessments, culture alignment, wellness interventions, and more, helping businesses optimize performance and achieve strategic goals.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR &amp; Admin Manager

Job Location: Asaba, Delta
Employment Type: Full-time
Resumption: Immediate.

Job Summary


	Develop and implement recruitment strategies to attract qualified candidates.
	Manage the full recruitment cycle (job posting, screening, interviewing, selection, and onboarding).
	Conduct employee induction and orientation programs.
	Maintain up-to-date employee records and HR database.
	Serve as a point of contact for employee concerns and workplace issues.
	Promote a positive work culture aligned with company values.
	Manage grievance procedures and conflict resolution.
	Plan employee engagement initiatives and welfare programs.
	Coordinate performance appraisal processes.
	Support managers in setting KPIs and monitoring performance.
	Identify training needs and coordinate learning &amp; development programs.
	Administer payroll inputs and ensure accuracy.
	Manage leave records and attendance tracking.
	Oversee benefits administration (health insurance, pensions, etc.).
	Ensure compliance with compensation policies and statutory requirements.
	Develop, review, and update HR policies and procedures.
	Ensure compliance with labor laws and regulatory standards.
	Prepare HR reports and metrics for management.
	Maintain confidentiality of employee information.
	Oversee daily office operations and ensure smooth workflow.
	Manage office supplies, assets, and vendor relationships.
	Supervise administrative staff (receptionist, office assistant, drivers, etc.).
	Coordinate facility maintenance and repairs.
	Maintain corporate records and official documentation.
	Prepare administrative and HR reports.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Time-to-fill vacancies.
	Employee retention rate.
	Compliance audit results.
	Employee engagement scores.
	Efficiency of administrative operations.
	Budget adherence.


Qualifications &amp; Experience


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or related field.
	7 &ndash; 9 years of HR and administrative experience (adjust based on level).
	Professional certification (CIPM, SHRM, HRCI) is compulsory.
	Strong knowledge of labour laws and HR best practices.
	Experience managing administrative operations.
	Asaba residents preferred
	Above 35 years.


Salary
N400,000 - N500,000 monthly (Negotiable).

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: jobs@pausefactory.org using &quot;Application&ndash; HR &amp; Admin Asaba&quot; as the subject of the email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-06-01",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Pause Factory"
    
  },

HR & Admin Manager Job at Pause Factory




        
    

        
        Pause Factory is a business and management consulting company that leverages people’s emotions and performance architecture to solve organizational challenges and craft organizational transformation. We provide innovative corporate training solutions, recruitment, HR outsourcing, organizational assessments, culture alignment, wellness interventions, and more, helping businesses optimize performance and achieve strategic goals.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR & Admin Manager

Job Location: Asaba, Delta
Employment Type: Full-time
Resumption: Immediate.

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Develop and implement recruitment strategies to attract qualified candidates.
	Manage the full recruitment cycle (job posting, screening, interviewing, selection, and onboarding).
	Conduct employee induction and orientation programs.
	Maintain up-to-date employee records and HR database.
	Serve as a point of contact for employee concerns and workplace issues.
	Promote a positive work culture aligned with company values.
	Manage grievance procedures and conflict resolution.
	Plan employee engagement initiatives and welfare programs.
	Coordinate performance appraisal processes.
	Support managers in setting KPIs and monitoring performance.
	Identify training needs and coordinate learning & development programs.
	Administer payroll inputs and ensure accuracy.
	Manage leave records and attendance tracking.
	Oversee benefits administration (health insurance, pensions, etc.).
	Ensure compliance with compensation policies and statutory requirements.
	Develop, review, and update HR policies and procedures.
	Ensure compliance with labor laws and regulatory standards.
	Prepare HR reports and metrics for management.
	Maintain confidentiality of employee information.
	Oversee daily office operations and ensure smooth workflow.
	Manage office supplies, assets, and vendor relationships.
	Supervise administrative staff (receptionist, office assistant, drivers, etc.).
	Coordinate facility maintenance and repairs.
	Maintain corporate records and official documentation.
	Prepare administrative and HR reports.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Time-to-fill vacancies.
	Employee retention rate.
	Compliance audit results.
	Employee engagement scores.
	Efficiency of administrative operations.
	Budget adherence.


Qualifications & Experience


	Bachelor’s Degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or related field.
	7 – 9 years of HR and administrative experience (adjust based on level).
	Professional certification (CIPM, SHRM, HRCI) is compulsory.
	Strong knowledge of labour laws and HR best practices.
	Experience managing administrative operations.
	Asaba residents preferred
	Above 35 years.


Salary
N400,000 - N500,000 monthly (Negotiable).

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Application– HR & Admin Asaba" as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "DevOps Engineer",
  "description": "ResumeSurge - We connect ambitious businesses with exceptional talent. We are a forward-thinking recruitment consultancy dedicated to helping companies find the right people to drive their success &mdash; and helping skilled professionals find the right opportunities to advance their careers.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: DevOps Engineer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The DevOps Infrastructure Engineer is responsible for building, automating, securing, and maintaining scalable cloud and on-premise infrastructure environments that support business-critical applications and services.
	The role focuses on infrastructure automation, CI/CD enablement, system reliability, monitoring, and cloud operations to ensure high availability, performance, and operational efficiency across the organization&rsquo;s technology platforms.
	The ideal candidate combines strong infrastructure engineering expertise with DevOps practices, cloud technologies, and automation capabilities to support rapid and reliable software delivery.


Key Responsibilities
Infrastructure &amp; Cloud Operations:


	Design, deploy, manage, and optimize cloud and hybrid infrastructure environments across AWS, Azure, or GCP.
	Maintain highly available, scalable, and resilient infrastructure platforms.
	Manage Linux and Windows server environments, networking, storage, and virtualization platforms.
	Implement infrastructure best practices for scalability, reliability, and fault tolerance.


DevOps &amp; Automation:


	Build and maintain Infrastructure as Code (IaC) using Terraform, Ansible, CloudFormation, or similar tools.
	Develop and optimize CI/CD pipelines for automated application deployment and infrastructure provisioning.
	Automate routine operational tasks, deployments, monitoring, and system configurations.
	Support containerized environments using Docker and orchestration platforms such as Kubernetes.


Monitoring, Reliability &amp; Incident Management:


	Implement and maintain monitoring, logging, and alerting systems using tools such as Prometheus, Grafana, ELK Stack, Zabbix, or Datadog.
	Ensure proactive detection and resolution of infrastructure and application issues.
	Participate in incident response, root cause analysis, and system recovery activities.
	Maintain backup, disaster recovery, and business continuity solutions.


Security &amp; Compliance:


	Implement infrastructure security best practices including patching, hardening, vulnerability remediation, and access control management.
	Ensure compliance with organizational security standards and regulatory requirements.
	Collaborate with Security teams to improve platform security posture.


Collaboration &amp; Documentation:


	Work closely with Software Engineering, Security, QA, and Product teams to support reliable software delivery.
	Maintain technical documentation for infrastructure architecture, deployment processes, configurations, and operational procedures.
	Support continuous improvement initiatives across infrastructure and DevOps practices.


Job Requirements
Education &amp; Experience:


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering, or related discipline.
	4&ndash;7+ years of experience in DevOps, Infrastructure Engineering, Cloud Engineering, or Platform Engineering roles.
	Relevant certifications such as:
	
		AWS Certified Solutions Architect
		AWS DevOps Engineer
		Microsoft Azure Administrator
		Kubernetes certifications (CKA/CKAD)
		Terraform Associate
		Red Hat Certified Engineer (RHCE).
	
	


Salary
N500,000 - N900,000 monthly.",
  "datePosted": "2026-05-12",
  "validThrough": "2026-06-13",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "ResumeSurge"
    
  },

DevOps Engineer Job at ResumeSurge




        
    

        
        ResumeSurge - We connect ambitious businesses with exceptional talent. We are a forward-thinking recruitment consultancy dedicated to helping companies find the right people to drive their success — and helping skilled professionals find the right opportunities to advance their careers.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: DevOps Engineer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The DevOps Infrastructure Engineer is responsible for building, automating, securing, and maintaining scalable cloud and on-premise infrastructure environments that support business-critical applications and services.
	The role focuses on infrastructure automation, CI/CD enablement, system reliability, monitoring, and cloud operations to ensure high availability, performance, and operational efficiency across the organization’s technology platforms.
	The ideal candidate combines strong infrastructure engineering expertise with DevOps practices, cloud technologies, and automation capabilities to support rapid and reliable software delivery.


Key Responsibilities
Infrastructure & Cloud Operations:


	Design, deploy, manage, and optimize cloud and hybrid infrastructure environments across AWS, Azure, or GCP.
	Maintain highly available, scalable, and resilient infrastructure platforms.
	Manage Linux and Windows server environments, networking, storage, and virtualization platforms.
	Implement infrastructure best practices for scalability, reliability, and fault tolerance.


DevOps & Automation:


	Build and maintain Infrastructure as Code (IaC) using Terraform, Ansible, CloudFormation, or similar tools.
	Develop and optimize CI/CD pipelines for automated application deployment and infrastructure provisioning.
	Automate routine operational tasks, deployments, monitoring, and system configurations.
	Support containerized environments using Docker and orchestration platforms such as Kubernetes.


Monitoring, Reliability & Incident Management:


	Implement and maintain monitoring, logging, and alerting systems using tools such as Prometheus, Grafana, ELK Stack, Zabbix, or Datadog.
	Ensure proactive detection and resolution of infrastructure and application issues.
	Participate in incident response, root cause analysis, and system recovery activities.
	Maintain backup, disaster recovery, and business continuity solutions.


Security & Compliance:


	Implement infrastructure security best practices including patching, hardening, vulnerability remediation, and access control management.
	Ensure compliance with organizational security standards and regulatory requirements.
	Collaborate with Security teams to improve platform security posture.


Collaboration & Documentation:


	Work closely with Software Engineering, Security, QA, and Product teams to support reliable software delivery.
	Maintain technical documentation for infrastructure architecture, deployment processes, configurations, and operational procedures.
	Support continuous improvement initiatives across infrastructure and DevOps practices.


Job Requirements
Education & Experience:


	Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering, or related discipline.
	4–7+ years of experience in DevOps, Infrastructure Engineering, Cloud Engineering, or Platform Engineering roles.
	Relevant certifications such as:
	
		AWS Certified Solutions Architect
		AWS DevOps Engineer
		Microsoft Azure Administrator
		Kubernetes certifications (CKA/CKAD)
		Terraform Associate
		Red Hat Certified Engineer (RHCE).
	
	


Salary
N500,000 - N900,000 monthly.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",

  "description": "Heavy Machinery Dealership (HMD) Limited - Incorporated in August 2003, HMD has built a strong reputation as a specialized distributor and service provider of premium heavy machinery and parts used in the mining, quarrying, construction, roadworks, recycling, transportation, and agricultural industries. Job Position: Warehouse Assistant

Job Location: Epe (Alaro City), Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Warehouse Assistant is responsible for supporting warehouse operations in a heavy machinery and equipment environment.
	The role involves receiving, storing, issuing, and maintaining heavy equipment parts, tools, and materials while ensuring accuracy, safety, and efficiency in all warehouse activities.


Key Responsibilities


	Receive, inspect, and verify incoming machinery parts, tools and equipment against delivery notes and purchase orders.
	Load and unload equipment parts and materials safely using appropriate handling equipment.
	Organize and store spare parts, components, and consumables in designated warehouse locations.
	Pick, pack, and prepare parts and materials for dispatch to sites, workshops, or customers.
	Maintain accurate inventory records and update stock movement in the warehouse management system.
	Conduct routine stock counts and assist in periodic inventory audits.
	Monitor stock levels and report shortages, damages, or discrepancies to the Warehouse Supervisor.
	Ensure proper labeling and identification of machinery parts and equipment.
	Maintain cleanliness, orderliness, and safety within the warehouse and storage areas.
	Adhere strictly to health, safety, and environmental (HSE) policies and procedures.
	Assist technicians and engineers in locating and issuing required spare parts and tools.
	Operate warehouse equipment such as forklifts, pallet jacks, and lifting devices where authorized.


Requirements &amp; Qualifications


	Minimum of OND or equivalent qualification.
	Previous warehouse or storekeeping experience, preferably in a heavy machinery, construction, engineering, or industrial environment.
	Basic knowledge of machinery spare parts and warehouse operations is an advantage.
	Ability to lift and handle heavy materials safely.
	Good organizational and record-keeping skills.
	Attention to detail and accuracy in inventory handling.
	Basic computer knowledge, especially inventory or ERP systems.
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
	Good communication skills.


Working Conditions:


	Warehouse and industrial work environment.
	Exposure to heavy equipment, machinery parts, and industrial materials.
	Requires standing, lifting, bending, and moving items regularly.


Key Competencies:


	Inventory Management
	Safety Awareness
	Teamwork and Collaboration
	Time Management
	Problem-Solving
	Attention to Detail.

Warehouse Assistant Job at Heavy Machinery Dealership




        
    

        
        Heavy Machinery Dealership (HMD) Limited - Incorporated in August 2003, HMD has built a strong reputation as a specialized distributor and service provider of premium heavy machinery and parts used in the mining, quarrying, construction, roadworks, recycling, transportation, and agricultural industries. HMD is focused on playing an integral part in the building of Africa’s infrastructure, where we have clearly positioned ourselves as the ultimate machinery solutions partner.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



HMD’s success is driven by its team of experts whose passion is to provide genuine after-sales support. The core of HMD’s business is to understand customer needs and to provide quality solutions and services with professionalism and integrity. We strive not only to succeed but rather to be of value. Our ultimate goal is to uplift the machinery industry across Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Warehouse Assistant

Job Location: Epe (Alaro City), Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Warehouse Assistant is responsible for supporting warehouse operations in a heavy machinery and equipment environment.
	The role involves receiving, storing, issuing, and maintaining heavy equipment parts, tools, and materials while ensuring accuracy, safety, and efficiency in all warehouse activities.


Key Responsibilities


	Receive, inspect, and verify incoming machinery parts, tools and equipment against delivery notes and purchase orders.
	Load and unload equipment parts and materials safely using appropriate handling equipment.
	Organize and store spare parts, components, and consumables in designated warehouse locations.
	Pick, pack, and prepare parts and materials for dispatch to sites, workshops, or customers.
	Maintain accurate inventory records and update stock movement in the warehouse management system.
	Conduct routine stock counts and assist in periodic inventory audits.
	Monitor stock levels and report shortages, damages, or discrepancies to the Warehouse Supervisor.
	Ensure proper labeling and identification of machinery parts and equipment.
	Maintain cleanliness, orderliness, and safety within the warehouse and storage areas.
	Adhere strictly to health, safety, and environmental (HSE) policies and procedures.
	Assist technicians and engineers in locating and issuing required spare parts and tools.
	Operate warehouse equipment such as forklifts, pallet jacks, and lifting devices where authorized.


Requirements & Qualifications


	Minimum of OND or equivalent qualification.
	Previous warehouse or storekeeping experience, preferably in a heavy machinery, construction, engineering, or industrial environment.
	Basic knowledge of machinery spare parts and warehouse operations is an advantage.
	Ability to lift and handle heavy materials safely.
	Good organizational and record-keeping skills.
	Attention to detail and accuracy in inventory handling.
	Basic computer knowledge, especially inventory or ERP systems.
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
	Good communication skills.


Working Conditions:


	Warehouse and industrial work environment.
	Exposure to heavy equipment, machinery parts, and industrial materials.
	Requires standing, lifting, bending, and moving items regularly.


Key Competencies:


	Inventory Management
	Safety Awareness
	Teamwork and Collaboration
	Time Management
	Problem-Solving
	Attention to Detail.


Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.
